en-us.html:
Recruits are the backbone of the Ordis military.

Story

I pass Gulrang, who is frowning as usual. I don't know what her problem is, but every time I walk into a room, I'm met with an ice-cold draught that comes straight from her direction. No matter. If she has a bone to pick with me, we'll have the chance to sort it out in the field. I turn to Commodore Jeanty and smile politely while introducing a recruit called Nosk. Nosk gives me a bored look, and I understand why. This is clearly a dreadful waste of time, but propriety and civility must be maintained, of course. The general staff want what they want. You just need to play by the rules, smile at the jokes and bow when it's expected. If they want to preen under the pretext of boosting the troops' morale, so be it.

I wink at Sunisa as I pass her. I see my aide-de-camp roll her eyes and suppress a smile. Because of her impressive performance, I put in a personal request to Admiral Singh to have her as my assistant. But it was her impertinence that really won me over. I was unsure at first. With the top-of-the-class aura she gave off, I didn't know if we would get along. But it turned out she always had an answer to everything, and was certainly never at a loss for words. That inspired me, and it was clear from that moment on that we had excellent compatibility in the field. I needed someone I could fully trust and who was independent enough to know what needed to be done without my input. And on top of that, Wingspan liked her too.

blocks.text: Recruits are the backbone of the Ordis military.

blocks.title: Story
paragraphs: I pass Gulrang, who is frowning as usual. I don't know what her problem is, but every time I walk into a room, I'm met with an ice-cold draught that comes straight from her direction. No matter. If she has a bone to pick with me, we'll have the chance to sort it out in the field. I turn to Commodore Jeanty and smile politely while introducing a recruit called Nosk. Nosk gives me a bored look, and I understand why. This is clearly a dreadful waste of time, but propriety and civility must be maintained, of course. The general staff want what they want. You just need to play by the rules, smile at the jokes and bow when it's expected. If they want to preen under the pretext of boosting the troops' morale, so be it.

I wink at Sunisa as I pass her. I see my aide-de-camp roll her eyes and suppress a smile. Because of her impressive performance, I put in a personal request to Admiral Singh to have her as my assistant. But it was her impertinence that really won me over. I was unsure at first. With the top-of-the-class aura she gave off, I didn't know if we would get along. But it turned out she always had an answer to everything, and was certainly never at a loss for words. That inspired me, and it was clear from that moment on that we had excellent compatibility in the field. fr-fr.html:
Les recrues sont la charpente des forces Ordis.

Histoire

Je passe à côté de Gulrang, qui fronce les sourcils comme à son habitude. Je ne sais pas ce qui cloche chez elle, mais à chaque fois que je rentre dans une pièce, c'est comme un courant d'air glacial qui m'accueille par son entremise. Mais soit, si elle veut en découdre, nous aurons l'occasion de régler ça au-delà des portes. Je me tourne vers le Commodore Jeanty, et souris avec politesse en lui présentant une recrue du nom de Nosk. Ce dernier me dévisage d'un air blasé, et je le comprends. Objectivement, c'est une affreuse perte de temps, mais il faut bien maintenir les convenances et les civilités. Les souhaits de l'état-major sont ce qu'ils sont. Il n'y a qu'à suivre le courant, sourire aux plaisanteries et faire quelques courbettes quand c'est nécessaire. S'il veut se faire mousser sous prétexte de maintenir le moral des troupes, qu'il en soit ainsi.

Je fais un clin d'œil à Sunisa en passant à côté d'elle. Je vois mon aide de camp lever les yeux au ciel et réprimer un sourire. J'ai personnellement demandé à l'Amiral Singh qu'elle devienne mon assistante, à la vue de ses performances. Mais c'est surtout son impertinence qui m'a séduit. J'étais dubitatif au début. Avec ses airs de première de la classe, je ne savais pas si nous nous entendrions bien. Mais force était de constater qu'elle avait du répondant, et était loin d'avoir sa langue dans sa poche. C'était quelque chose qui me stimulait, au final, et il était clair dorénavant que notre compatibilité sur le terrain était optimale. Il me fallait une personne en qui je puisse avoir entièrement confiance, suffisamment autonome pour savoir ce qui devait être fait sans que j'aie à intervenir. Et qui plus est, Wingspan l'aimait bien, lui aussi.
